_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Identifying complex problems and reviewing related information to develop and evaluate options and implement solutions.
reference_document_element_description: Ability to synthesize risk information from multiple sources and assessment methodologies into coherent risk narratives that support executive decision-making.
comments: Complex problem solving is the core cognitive skill underlying all WIDAI analytical and decision-making KSAs. WIDAI expresses it through domain-specific problem-solving: risk synthesis (RM-A-003), analytical study design (AB-S-007), architecture trade-off analysis (DA-K-001), governance calibration (AG-A-001). O*NET is the general capability.
_comment_atlas_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: Moderate similarity (nist band: roughly as similar as dissimilar). The STS score reflects meaningful semantic overlap between the broader O*NET element and the narrower WIDAI KSA. The superset relationship is well-supported — the shared concepts are substantial enough for the model to detect cross-text alignment.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: F1 of 0.8687 indicates moderate token-level semantic alignment. Precision and Recall are balanced, indicating roughly symmetric semantic coverage between the two descriptions. Balanced P/R for a Superset is plausible when the O*NET element's broader scope uses vocabulary that diverges from WIDAI's domain-specific terms, compressing both measures toward similar values.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: Neutral-dominant (96.99%) — the model finds the descriptions related but neither implying nor contradicting each other. Neutral is common for Superset pairs where the scope gap is large — the FDE covers broader territory than the KSA, so the NLI model sees topical relatedness without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: The bi-encoder score (0.3569) is moderately lower than the STS cross-encoder (0.5070). The gap (0.15) indicates the relationship has a cross-abstraction component — some of the semantic alignment requires joint processing to detect, suggesting vocabulary or scope divergence between the two descriptions.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Low lexical overlap (10.34% of tokens shared). Some shared vocabulary exists (likely common functional words or broadly-used domain terms) but the bulk of each description uses distinct terminology.
